concerns.text: FR-008 and Constitution Principle VI require recording peak RAM, CPU utilization, and inference time for *every* batch. T006 only sets up the logging infrastructure ('Configure logging... to log RAM usage...'). There is no specific task to implement the actual collection logic (e.g., `psutil` calls) and recording within the inference loop. T020 mentions monitoring but lacks explicit CPU utilization tracking required by FR-008.

FR-010 mandates flagging predictors with VIF ≥ 5 for 'orthogonalization or exclusion'. T024 implements the exclusion step ('excludes predictors with VIF ≥ 5'), but there is no task to implement the 'orthogonalization' fallback path or the specific flagging mechanism required by the spec if exclusion is not chosen.

FR-003 requires generating a 'deterministic baseline set' of smell labels. T009 only mentions 'Implement Pylint execution'. It lacks an explicit task to implement the 'normalization to canonical smell names' described in plan.md (Complexity Tracking) which is required to make the baseline deterministic and comparable against LLM labels.

SC-005 requires validity confirmation for ≥ 95% of the *sampled* functions (merged dataset). T012 validates `static_baseline.csv` (US1) and T029 validates `results/` artifacts (US3). Neither task explicitly combines data from US1 and US2 to verify the *merged* dataset validity against the 95% threshold before statistical analysis, risking analysis on incomplete data.
